demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged care facility; requires maximal assistance with activities of daily living

contact_nok.relationship: facility nursing staff (present and reporting on the resident's behalf)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I am completely exhausted, my skin is itching everywhere, and I feel sick to my stomach all the time. (Facility nurse adds: she's gone downhill over the past month, her legs have swollen right up, and the last few days she's barely eaten or drunk anything because the nausea is so bad.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Facility staff called after-hours because the resident has become profoundly tired, her legs are increasingly swollen, and relentless nausea has stopped her eating and drinking over the last few days.

symptom_narrative.onset: gradual over the past month, with a clear worsening in the last few days
symptom_narrative.duration: about a month of tiredness; swelling for two weeks; nausea and not eating for the last four days
symptom_narrative.character: overwhelming tiredness and heaviness, constant queasiness with no appetite, and an all-over itch of the skin with no rash
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: she says she has never felt this drained; the nausea is bad enough that she cannot face any food
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: swelling in both legs from the ankles up toward the knees; itching all over the body; queasiness in the stomach
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and slowly getting worse; leg cramps come on in the evenings; she is up several times at night to pass water
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing she or the staff have tried has helped
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it has been steadily worsening on its own; she cannot keep food or much fluid down
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: poor appetite and almost no intake, night-time leg cramps, getting up often at night to pass urine, and constant scratching; she denies vomiting and denies feeling short of breath
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: staff report a long, slow decline rather than a single previous episode like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: now needs maximal help with everything; too exhausted to get out of bed unaided

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: high blood pressure, treated with tablets
past_medical_history.how_managed: on regular blood-pressure tablets, but staff say her readings have been running high lately

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in an aged care facility with nursing support; frail and dependent for daily care; nursing staff are the informant for this encounter

objective_data_offered.timing: on the facility monitor this evening
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: facility cuff reading relayed by nursing staff; single reading

objective_data_offered.value: she looks pale and unwell, is repeatedly scratching her arms and chest, and both lower legs look visibly swollen
multiple untouched meal trays at the bedside and very little fluid taken in the last 24 hours
